---
title: "The Digital Choice Manifesto"
subtitle: "Reclaiming Agency Through Structural Realism and Local Compute"
author: "Yichao Jin, Ph.D."
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
---

> "Modern algorithms treat human choice as a series of data points to be exploited. We treat it as a structural architecture to be empowered."

## The Vision
In an era of centralized algorithmic governance, individual agency is often lost in the "black box" of predictive modeling. This Manifesto advocates for a paradigm shift...

## The Three Pillars

### 1. Structural Realism
We reject superficial correlations. By utilizing TRIBE-v2, we recover the latent structural parameters of human decision-making: Beta (Present Bias), Delta (Trust), and Kappa (Friction).

### 2. Privacy Sovereignty
We champion "Privacy by Architecture." By leveraging local compute (Apple Silicon/MLX), we ensure that high-fidelity behavioral simulations occur entirely on the user's edge device, requiring zero cloud exposure.

### 3. Cognitive Equity
We define "Administrative Sludge" as a hidden tax on human potential. Our mission is to identify and eliminate the systemic frictions that disproportionately exclude the 45% Hidden Majority from social and economic progress.

[Explore Clinical Applications →](/clinical/) | [Explore Platform Economics →](/platforms/)
